content:
# Joshua Chapter 14 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 237-238 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_014_page_001.jpg`

---

1 And these [are] what the sons of Yisra'el inherited in the land of Kena'an, which El'azar the priest and Yehoshua son of Nun and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the sons of Yisra'el gave as inheritance to them,

2 by the lot of their inheritance, as YHWH had commanded by the hand of Moshe, for the nine tribes and the half-tribe.

3 For Moshe had given the inheritance of the two tribes and the half-tribe beyond the Yarden; and to the Levi'im he had not given an inheritance in their midst.

4 For the sons of Yosef were two tribes, Menasheh and Efrayim; and they did not give a portion to the Levi'im in the land, except cities to dwell in and their open pastures for their livestock and for their possessions.

5 As YHWH had commanded Moshe, so the sons of Yisra'el did, and they divided the land.

6 And the sons of Yehudah drew near to Yehoshua in Gilgal, and Kalev son of Yefuneh the Qenizzi said to him: "You know the word that YHWH spoke to Moshe the man of God concerning me and concerning you in Qadesh Barnea.

7 [I was] a son of forty years when Moshe the servant of YHWH sent me from Qadesh Barnea to spy out the land, and I brought back word to him as [it was] with my heart.

8 And my brothers who went up with me made the heart of the people melt, but I filled [myself] after YHWH my God.

9 And Moshe swore on that day, saying: 'Surely the land upon which your foot has trodden shall be for an inheritance to you and to your sons unto eternity, because you filled [yourself] after YHWH my God.'

10 And now, behold, YHWH has kept me alive, as he spoke, these forty and five years from the time YHWH spoke this word to Moshe, when Yisra'el walked in the wilderness; and now, behold, I [am] today a son of eighty and five years.

11 I [am] still today as strong as [on] the day Moshe sent me; as my strength [was] then, so [is] my strength now, for war and for going out and for coming in.

12 And now, give to me this hill-country of which YHWH spoke on that day, for you heard on that day that Anaqim [are] there and cities great [and] fortified. Perhaps YHWH [will be] with me and I shall dispossess them, as YHWH spoke."

13 And Yehoshua blessed him, and he gave Chevron to Kalev son of Yefuneh for an inheritance.

14 Therefore Chevron became [the inheritance] of Kalev son of Yefuneh the Qenizzi for an inheritance unto this day, because he filled [himself] after YHWH the God of Yisra'el.

15 And the name of Chevron formerly [was] Qiryat Arba; he [was] the great man among the Anaqim. And the land had rest from war.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v1 — Inheritance formula:** The Hebrew וְאֵלֶּה אֲשֶׁר־נָחֲלוּ ("And these [are] what they inherited") opens the second major division of Joshua (chs. 14-21), the distribution of the land. The verb נָחַל (nachal) carries the specific sense of taking possession of an allotted inheritance, distinct from יָרַשׁ (yarash, "to dispossess/possess").

2. **v2 — בְּגוֹרַל נַחֲלָתָם (beghoral nachalatam):** "By the lot of their inheritance" — the lot (גּוֹרָל, goral) was the divinely sanctioned mechanism for distributing the land (cf. Num 26:55-56, 33:54). The phrase emphasizes that the allocation was by YHWH's determination, not human preference.

3. **v3 — Beyond the Yarden:** The Hebrew מֵעֵבֶר לַיַּרְדֵּן ("beyond the Yarden") refers to the Transjordanian territory east of the river, already allocated by Moshe (Num 32; Deut 3:12-17). The Levi'im exclusion clause (וְלַלְוִיִּם לֹא־נָתַן נַחֲלָה) is restated from Numbers 18:20-24.

4. **v4 — Two tribes from Yosef:** The explanation that Yosef's sons formed two tribes (Menasheh and Efrayim) resolves the arithmetic: twelve tribal territories exist despite Levi receiving none, because Yosef's single allotment was divided in two. The Levi'im received cities and מִגְרְשֵׁיהֶם (migrsheihem, "their open pastures") rather than contiguous territory.

5. **v6 — Kalev's speech:** Kalev son of Yefuneh is identified as הַקְּנִזִּי (haqqenizzi, "the Qenizzi"), connecting him to the Qenizzite clan (cf. Gen 15:19; Num 32:12). This ethnic designation suggests non-Israelite origins adopted into Yehudah. The setting at Gilgal places this before the move to Shiloh (18:1).

6. **v7 — בֶּן־אַרְבָּעִים שָׁנָה (ben-arba'im shanah):** "A son of forty years" — the Hebrew age formula uses בֶּן (ben, "son of") followed by the number, a characteristic idiom preserved literally. Similarly in v. 10: בֶּן־חָמֵשׁ וּשְׁמוֹנִים שָׁנָה ("a son of eighty and five years"), yielding eighty-five.

7. **v8 — מִלֵּא אַחֲרֵי (mille' acharei):** "Filled [myself] after" — this idiom, recurring in vv. 8, 9, and 14, translates the Piel of מָלֵא (male', "to fill") with the preposition אַחֲרֵי ("after"). The expression means to follow completely, wholeheartedly. Most translations render "followed fully" or "wholly followed," but the literal root sense of "filling after" is preserved here to retain the Hebrew metaphor of fullness of devotion.

8. **v8 — הִמְסוּ אֶת־לֵבַב הָעָם (himsu et-levav ha'am):** "Made the heart of the people melt" — the Hiphil of מָסָס (masas) describes the demoralizing effect of the ten spies' evil report (Num 13:31-33; Deut 1:28). The same verb appears in Josh 2:11 and 5:1 describing Canaanite fear.

9. **v10 — Chronological note:** Kalev's arithmetic: he was forty at the spying of Qadesh Barnea (Num 13, in the second year of the wilderness), and now forty-five years later he is eighty-five. This places the beginning of the land distribution approximately seven years after the crossing of the Yarden (forty years of wandering minus two years before the spying, plus seven years of conquest).

10. **v12 — אוּלַי (ulai):** "Perhaps" — Kalev's use of this conditional particle is striking. Despite YHWH's promise, Kalev speaks with reverent contingency: "Perhaps YHWH [will be] with me." This is not doubt but pious deference, acknowledging that YHWH's assistance depends on YHWH's sovereign will. The Anaqim (עֲנָקִים) are the giant inhabitants who terrified the original spies (Num 13:28, 33).

11. **v13 — Chevron:** The Hebrew חֶבְרוֹן (Chevron) is given to Kalev as his personal inheritance within the tribal territory of Yehudah. The verb וַיְבָרֲכֵהוּ ("and he blessed him") indicates Yehoshua's formal bestowing of the grant, not merely a verbal blessing.

12. **v14 — עַל־כֵּן (al-ken):** "Therefore" — this summary formula explains the etiological reason for Kalev's possession of Chevron: his complete devotion to YHWH. The phrase עַד הַיּוֹם הַזֶּה ("unto this day") is a formulaic marker indicating the situation persisted to the time of the narrative's composition or editing.

13. **v15 — Qiryat Arba:** The Hebrew קִרְיַת אַרְבַּע (Qiryat Arba, "City of Arba") is the ancient name of Chevron. The clause הָאָדָם הַגָּדוֹל בָּעֲנָקִים הוּא ("he [was] the great man among the Anaqim") identifies Arba as the greatest of the Anaqim. The final clause וְהָאָרֶץ שָׁקְטָה מִמִּלְחָמָה ("And the land had rest from war") is a recurring formula in Joshua (cf. 11:23) marking the conclusion of a narrative unit.

14. **LXX note:** No Vaticanus LXX images were available for this chapter. Key known variants: the LXX adds "and Kalev was with him" at the end of v. 6 in some traditions, and renders the Anaqim as γίγαντες (gigantes, "giants") or Ενακιμ depending on the manuscript tradition. The LXX at v. 15 reads πόλις Αρβοκ μητρόπολις τῶν Ενακιμ ("city of Arbok, mother-city of the Enakeim"), interpreting הָאָדָם הַגָּדוֹל differently from the MT.
